methodology: Theory-derived predictions on datasets never used in any prior experiment

predictions.A: Per-pair Gaussian flip Φ(-SNR) will achieve R² > 0.80 on EACH dataset
predictions.B: Coverage conflict (frac SNR < 0.5) will rank-correlate with mean instability across the 10 datasets at Spearman ρ > 0.60
predictions.C: Within-group flip > between-group flip on ≥7/10 datasets (directional prediction at ρ*=0.70)
predictions.D: DASH (orbit average) will produce a more stable ranking than the median single model on ≥8/10 datasets
predictions.E: The number of stable pairwise facts (flip < 0.05) will correlate with g(g-1)/2 at Spearman ρ > 0.70

why_these_predictions.A: Φ(-SNR) is proved to be the flip rate under Gaussian noise. 94.7% of real SHAP differences are Gaussian. R²>0.80 is conservative (observed 0.95-0.98 on 6 datasets).
why_these_predictions.B: Coverage conflict = fraction of pairs in the bilemma zone. Validated at ρ=0.96 on 15 datasets. ρ>0.60 is very conservative.
why_these_predictions.C: Directional prediction confirmed at p=5.1e-11 on 149 datasets. 7/10 is conservative (81% directional on 110 datasets).
why_these_predictions.D: DASH is proved Pareto-optimal. Empirically reduces instability on every tested dataset.
why_these_predictions.E: The stable fact count = g(g-1)/2 follows from the capacity theorem.
